Mithril.js — vendored ESM build
================================

Upstream:   https://mithril.js.org/
Version:    2.3.8
Source URL: https://unpkg.com/mithril@2.3.8/mithril.js  (UMD)
Reviewed:   2026-06-09
License:    MIT

Upstream UMD SHA-256:
  6c080d9a4b6289e32534bc2a0bab94cc864c5206f106bae4f8de96f4900b9960

Vendored ESM SHA-256 (mithril.js as committed):
  6114f196bbfb7b857660860c69820d79123ca9a79929b2f149f4935e79ccc5ef

Vendored global SHA-256 (mithril.global.js as committed):
  8c0ad82fcb9d36ebfcd1b1e5d3350d578a4c7ca5f63ac3355015de264b0a549e

Notable changes since 2.2.2 (previous vendored version)
-------------------------------------------------------
- m.jsonp removed upstream — the API and its script-injection code no
  longer exist in the build at all (we already forbade it; see below).
- Internal Promise polyfill removed — request/redraw now use the native
  global Promise. Smaller build; assumes a modern browser, which our
  MV3 target already guarantees.
- m.domFor added — new public API for iterating the DOM nodes a vnode
  rendered. Not used by us; not forbidden.

Conversion
----------
The upstream ships UMD. We convert to ESM at vendor time by stripping the
IIFE wrapper and the module/window export tail, then appending
`export default m;`. The body is byte-identical to upstream — see the
prefix/suffix asserts in scripts/vendor-update.md (or the original
conversion script invocation in the project history).

Companion file: mithril.global.js
----------------------------------
mithril.global.js is the CLASSIC/global twin of mithril.js, generated by
swapping the trailing ESM default-export for `window.m = m`. The side
panel and other extension code use the ESM mithril.js; sandboxed Apps
(opaque-origin iframes that can't resolve ES imports) load the global
build instead — app-tab injects it as the virtual `mithril.js` so an app
can `<script src="./mithril.js"></script>` and get `window.m`. Regenerate
it whenever mithril.js is updated (same one-line export swap).

Forbidden APIs (enforced by ESLint, see .eslintrc.cjs)
------------------------------------------------------
The following Mithril APIs are forbidden in this project. Importing or
referencing them is a lint error.

  m.request    — bypasses our safeFetch egress allowlist (§4.5)
  m.jsonp      — same reason; would also load remote scripts. As of 2.3.x
                 this API is gone from upstream entirely; the ESLint rule
                 is kept as a tripwire in case a future bump reintroduces it.
  m.mount(document.body, ...)
               — mount only against named container elements; see §13.2

Why we don't fork
-----------------
Stripping unused Mithril methods would save ~1-2KB but means owning a
diverged copy. The ESLint rules above make unused APIs unreachable in
practice; that is sufficient. See §13.3 of docs/design-v1.md.

Update procedure
----------------
1. Curl new UMD from unpkg.com/mithril@X.Y.Z/mithril.js
2. Re-run the conversion (see scripts/vendor-update.md)
3. Read the diff against upstream UMD; verify it is what you expect.
4. Record the new SHA-256s and date here.
5. Re-run the test suite against a representative side-panel build.
